lifecycle.decommission: Provider releases/deletes the volume; Discovered reflects released/absent. Deleting a volume destroys its data — so decommission is DATA-DESTRUCTIVE and must follow the workloads that attach it.

metadata.description: A CONSUMABLE persistent storage volume — the block or file volume a workload attaches, as distinct from the Storage.Cluster that PROVISIONS it (the platform) and the physical device that backs it. Vendor-neutral Tier-1: provisioners (Ceph RBD/CephFS, cloud CSI drivers, NFS) are the provider, never the type. Adopts Kubernetes PersistentVolumeClaim/CSI + SNIA Swordfish Volume.

context.purpose: Declares a consumable persistent volume — the block or file storage a workload attaches — independent of what provisions it.
context.plain_description: The unit of storage a workload asks for and attaches: requested capacity, how concurrently it may be attached (one writer, many readers, and so on), filesystem versus raw block, and which storage class provisions it. It is deliberately distinct from the platform that builds it and the devices that back it. Once realized, the provider's volume handle comes back, tying the request to the actual volume.
use_when: You need a workload's storage requested by capacity, access mode, and class, portable across provisioners.
You need volumes ordered in the graph: realized before their consumer, never outliving their provisioning cluster.
not_for: The provisioning policy — Platform.StorageClass; the volume references a class by name.
The storage platform — Storage.Cluster provisions volumes.
Host-local ZFS/LVM storage a host service mounts — Storage.Dataset.
works_with: Platform.StorageClass — the class declaring what kind of storage the volume gets.
Compute.VirtualMachine — the consumer(s) it attaches to.
Data.Database — databases whose data directory it backs.

capacity.description: Requested capacity, Kubernetes quantity form (e.g. 100Gi). SNIA Swordfish CapacityBytes maps here.

volume_mode.description: Filesystem-mounted vs raw block device (Kubernetes PVC volumeMode).

storage_class.description: Resource reference to a Platform.StorageClass (common-elements §2.5, ADR-025). Authored by handle; deferred resolution supported (claim-before-define).

oneOf.description: Short form — the handle of an existing Platform.StorageClass.

retain_on_release.description: Reclaim policy: true = Retain (keep data after the consumer releases it), false = Delete.

volume_handle.description: Provider volume handle/id once Realized (CSI volumeHandle / Swordfish Volume Id).

attached.description: Whether the volume is currently attached to a consumer.

relationships.name: provisioned_by
relationships.description: The Storage.Cluster that provisions this volume (binds its block_storage_class / file_storage_class output). A volume cannot outlive its provisioning cluster.

relationships.name: attaches_to
relationships.description: The consumer(s) this volume is attached to (RWX volumes attach to many). attaches_to is the same relation IPAddress uses for interface attachment — the consumable-resource-to-consumer edge.

adopts.role: consumable-volume request vocabulary (capacity, accessModes, volumeMode, storageClass, reclaimPolicy)
storage-volume-as-asset vocabulary (CapacityBytes, AccessCapabilities) — same family as Storage.Cluster
